topic: Work Burnout: Warning Signs to Watch For
content:
# Work Burnout: Warning Signs to Watch For

## Overview
Burnout often develops slowly and can be hard to notice at first. It usually shows up as emotional exhaustion, growing cynicism, and reduced effectiveness at work. People may think they are just tired or need to try harder, but burnout often reflects chronic stress that has gone on too long. Catching the warning signs early can make recovery easier.

## Key Concepts / Symptoms
Common signs include feeling drained even after rest, becoming more irritable, and feeling detached from work. A person may notice lower concentration, more mistakes, or less motivation. Tasks that once felt manageable may start to feel overwhelming. Burnout can also affect sleep, mood, appetite, and physical health.

## Coping Strategies
Pay attention to patterns rather than waiting for total exhaustion. Build in small breaks, protect time away from work, and reduce avoidable overload where possible. It may also help to talk to a supervisor about workload, priorities, or expectations. Simple recovery habits like sleep, movement, and limiting after-hours work can make a difference.

## When to Seek Support
If burnout is affecting mental health, physical health, or job performance, additional support may be needed. A therapist, doctor, or workplace support program can help assess next steps and prevent deeper exhaustion.
